question: In Wix, how do you edit the header title for the Experience section?
answer: Click the 'Edit' button in the top-left corner, select 'Header Title' from the 'Design' dropdown menu, then type your desired text into the 'Header Title' text field within the 'Experience' section.
keypoints: The user clicks on the 'Edit' button located in the top-left corner of the application window.
From the 'Design' dropdown menu, the 'Header Title' option is selected by a mouse click.
In the 'Experience' section, the user types 'Past Experience' into the text field labeled 'Header Title'.

question: In Wix, how do you change the subtitle setting and header layout style?
answer: Hover and click the 'Subtitle' option in the edit menu to enable it, then scroll through design options in the left-side panel and select a new layout style to update the header's visual appearance.
keypoints: The cursor hovers over the 'Subtitle' option within the edit menu and clicks it, enabling the subtitle feature for the header.
The user scrolls through various design options in the left-side panel and selects a new layout style from the list, changing the appearance of the header section.

question: In Wix, how do you apply a new design template to a section after accessing customization options?
answer: Right-click the desired label (e.g., 'Experience') to open its context menu, click the 'Design' tab at the top of the screen to view templates, then select a specific template to update the section's visual layout.
keypoints: A right-click action is performed on the 'Experience' label in the menu, causing a context menu to appear with additional customization options.
The user clicks on the 'Design' tab at the top of the screen, revealing different design templates that can be applied to the current section.
The user selects a specific design template from the displayed options, which updates the visual layout of the webpage accordingly.
